title: The Space Force is now seeking to buy up to $30 billion in rocket launches
excerpt: The Trump administration is asking the Space Force to do a lot. This will require more launches.
content_html:
It seems as though $5.6 billion wasn’t enough. That was the news from the US Space Force on Friday, when military officials announced they were tripling the maximum value of one of the service’s National Security Space Launch contracts to $17 billion.

The expansion of the Space Force’s National Security Space Launch (NSSL) Phase 3 contract comes as the Pentagon signals rising demand for military satellite launches. The NSSL program is set up to allow Space Systems Command, which oversees the Space Force’s launch program, to select from a pool of launch providers for individual missions to deliver the military’s satellites to orbit.

The NSSL program has two parts. Lane 1 covers the Space Force’s more risk-tolerant missions, such as medium-lift launches with experimental payloads or rideshare missions carrying satellites for the Pentagon’s surveillance or data relay constellations. Lane 2 includes higher-priority strategic missions, like the government’s largest and most expensive spy satellites, or radiation-hardened communications satellites designed to survive a nuclear war.

Lane 1 is open to commercial launch providers without requiring extensive certification and oversight from the Space Force. This is the part the Pentagon has now authorized for $17 billion in launch procurement expenses. Missions in Lane 2 can only launch on rockets that go through the military’s lengthy certification reviews. Only SpaceX’s Falcon 9 and Falcon Heavy, and United Launch Alliance’s Vulcan rocket are certified for Lane 2 missions.

More money for more launches

There are more launch companies qualified for Lane 1 contracts. Space Systems Command originally selected SpaceX, ULA, and Blue Origin for Lane 1 in the Phase 3 round of the NSSL program in 2024. Rocket Lab, Stoke Space, and most recently, Relativity Space and Impulse Space were added to the roster of companies competing to launch Lane 1 missions. The Space Force issues a request for bids on a number of Lane 1 missions each year, and then doles out individual fixed-price “task orders” to the winners. SpaceX has won the lion’s share of Lane 1 task orders to date, and Blue Origin won its first earlier this year.

sentences.zh: 这是美国太空军周五发布的消息，当时军方官员宣布，他们将该军种的一项国家安全太空发射合同的最高价值增加两倍，达到 170 亿美元。

Lane 1 covers the Space Force’s more risk-tolerant missions, such as medium-lift launches with experimental payloads or rideshare missions carrying satellites for the Pentagon’s surveillance or data relay constellations. Lane 2 includes higher-priority strategic missions, like the government’s largest and most expensive spy satellites, or radiation-hardened communications satellites designed to survive a nuclear war.

Lane 1 covers the Space Force’s more risk-tolerant missions, such as medium-lift launches with experimental payloads or rideshare missions carrying satellites for the Pentagon’s surveillance or data relay constellations.
sentences.zh: 第 1 道涵盖了太空军的风险承受能力更强的任务，例如带有实验有效载荷的中型运载火箭发射或为五角大楼的监视或数据中继星座运载卫星的拼车任务。

sentences.en: Lane 2 includes higher-priority strategic missions, like the government’s largest and most expensive spy satellites, or radiation-hardened communications satellites designed to survive a nuclear war.
sentences.zh: 2 号通道包括更高优先级的战略任务，例如政府最大、最昂贵的间谍卫星，或设计用于在核战争中生存的抗辐射通信卫星。

This is the part the Pentagon has now authorized for $17 billion in launch procurement expenses. Missions in Lane 2 can only launch on rockets that go through the military’s lengthy certification reviews. Only SpaceX’s Falcon 9 and Falcon Heavy, and United Launch Alliance’s Vulcan rocket are certified for Lane 2 missions.

sentences.zh: Lane 1 向商业发射提供商开放，无需太空军的广泛认证和监督。

sentences.en: This is the part the Pentagon has now authorized for $17 billion in launch procurement expenses.
sentences.zh: 这是五角大楼现已批准的 170 亿美元发射采购费用的一部分。

sentences.en: Missions in Lane 2 can only launch on rockets that go through the military’s lengthy certification reviews.
sentences.zh: 2 号通道的任务只能使用经过军方漫长认证审查的火箭发射。

sentences.en: Only SpaceX’s Falcon 9 and Falcon Heavy, and United Launch Alliance’s Vulcan rocket are certified for Lane 2 missions.
sentences.zh: 只有 SpaceX 的猎鹰 9 号和猎鹰重型火箭以及联合发射联盟的火神火箭获得了执行 Lane 2 任务的认证。
更多资金用于更多发射

blocks.html_en: There are more launch companies qualified for Lane 1 contracts. Space Systems Command originally selected SpaceX, ULA, and Blue Origin for Lane 1 in the Phase 3 round of the NSSL program in 2024. Rocket Lab, Stoke Space, and most recently, Relativity Space and Impulse Space were added to the roster of companies competing to launch Lane 1 missions. The Space Force issues a request for bids on a number of Lane 1 missions each year, and then doles out individual fixed-price “task orders” to the winners. SpaceX has won the lion’s share of Lane 1 task orders to date, and Blue Origin won its first earlier this year.

sentences.en: There are more launch companies qualified for Lane 1 contracts.
sentences.zh: 有更多的发射公司有资格获得 Lane 1 合同。

sentences.en: Space Systems Command originally selected SpaceX, ULA, and Blue Origin for Lane 1 in the Phase 3 round of the NSSL program in 2024.
sentences.zh: 太空系统司令部最初选择 SpaceX、ULA 和 Blue Origin 参与 2024 年 NSSL 项目第三阶段的 Lane 1。

sentences.en: Rocket Lab, Stoke Space, and most recently, Relativity Space and Impulse Space were added to the roster of companies competing to launch Lane 1 missions.
sentences.zh: Rocket Lab、Stoke Space，以及最近的 Relativity Space 和 Impulse Space 都被添加到竞争发射 Lane 1 任务的公司名单中。

sentences.en: The Space Force issues a request for bids on a number of Lane 1 missions each year, and then doles out individual fixed-price “task orders” to the winners.
sentences.zh: 天军每年都会对许多第一车道任务发出招标请求，然后向获胜者发放单独的固定价格“任务订单”。

sentences.en: SpaceX has won the lion’s share of Lane 1 task orders to date, and Blue Origin won its first earlier this year.
sentences.zh: 迄今为止，SpaceX 已赢得了 Lane 1 任务订单的大部分，而蓝色起源则在今年早些时候赢得了第一个任务订单。

content_html_zh:
56 亿美元似乎还不够。 这是美国太空军周五发布的消息，当时军方官员宣布，他们将该军种的一项国家安全太空发射合同的最高价值增加两倍，达到 170 亿美元。

太空部队国家安全太空发射（NSSL）第三阶段合同的扩展正值五角大楼发出信号，表明对军事卫星发射的需求不断增长。 NSSL 计划的设立是为了让负责监督太空部队发射计划的太空系统司令部从一系列发射提供商中进行选择，以执行将军事卫星送入轨道的个别任务。

NSSL 计划有两个部分。 第 1 道涵盖了太空军的风险承受能力更强的任务，例如带有实验有效载荷的中型运载火箭发射或为五角大楼的监视或数据中继星座运载卫星的拼车任务。 2 号通道包括更高优先级的战略任务，例如政府最大、最昂贵的间谍卫星，或设计用于在核战争中生存的抗辐射通信卫星。

Lane 1 向商业发射提供商开放，无需太空军的广泛认证和监督。 这是五角大楼现已批准的 170 亿美元发射采购费用的一部分。 2 号通道的任务只能使用经过军方漫长认证审查的火箭发射。 只有 SpaceX 的猎鹰 9 号和猎鹰重型火箭以及联合发射联盟的火神火箭获得了执行 Lane 2 任务的认证。

更多资金用于更多发射

有更多的发射公司有资格获得 Lane 1 合同。 太空系统司令部最初选择 SpaceX、ULA 和 Blue Origin 参与 2024 年 NSSL 项目第三阶段的 Lane 1。 Rocket Lab、Stoke Space，以及最近的 Relativity Space 和 Impulse Space 都被添加到竞争发射 Lane 1 任务的公司名单中。 天军每年都会对许多第一车道任务发出招标请求，然后向获胜者发放单独的固定价格“任务订单”。 迄今为止，SpaceX 已赢得了 Lane 1 任务订单的大部分，而蓝色起源则在今年早些时候赢得了第一个任务订单。
